title: Solar Installation Process
description: Step-by-step guide to what happens during a solar installation.
keyTakeaways: The process starts with design and site survey
Permits and interconnection can take time
Installation is often faster than approval
Utility permission to operate is required

sections.title: Site Survey and Design
sections.content: Installers inspect roof, electrical panel, shade, and layout before finalizing a design.

sections.title: Permits and Utility Review
sections.content: Your installer typically submits permits and interconnection paperwork.

sections.title: Installation and Activation
sections.content: After installation and inspection, the utility grants permission to operate.

faqs.question: How long does solar installation take?
faqs.answer: The physical installation may take a few days, but permitting and utility approval can take weeks or months.

sources: U.S. Department of Energy solar homeowner resources
IRS residential clean energy credit guidance
DSIRE state and utility incentive database
